title: Pane Selector Syntax
text:
Pane selectors MUST be strings conforming to one of the following formats:

| Syntax | Semantics |
|--------|-----------|
| `id:terminal:N` | Terminal pane with numeric ID `N`. |
| `id:plugin:N` | Plugin pane with numeric ID `N`. |
| `terminal:N` | Shorthand for `id:terminal:N`. |
| `plugin:N` | Shorthand for `id:plugin:N`. |
| `focused` | The currently focused pane in the active tab. |
| `title:<substring>` | All panes whose title contains `<substring>` (case-insensitive). |
| `title:/<regex>/` | All panes whose title matches the regex pattern. |
| `cmd:<substring>` | All panes whose running command contains `<substring>` (case-insensitive). |
| `cmd:/<regex>/` | All panes whose running command matches the regex pattern. |
| `tab:N:index:M` | The pane at position `M` (sorted by type then ID) in tab `N`. |

`N` and `M` MUST be non-negative integers. Leading zeros in numeric IDs MUST be accepted (e.g., `id:terminal:007` is equivalent to `id:terminal:7`).

Regex patterns MUST use Rust `regex` crate syntax. Implementations MUST validate regex patterns at parse time and return `invalid_params` for invalid patterns.

When a selector matches multiple panes and the operation does not support `all`, the plugin MUST return an `ambiguous_match` error. When a selector matches zero panes, the plugin MUST return a `no_match` error.

The `focused` selector MUST resolve using the current client's pane focus. If the current client's focus cannot be determined, the plugin SHOULD fall back to the focused terminal pane in the active tab.
